about.heading: About Me
about.tldr: I play nice, strive for quality, ask questions, and practice humility.
paragraphs: Having recently completed a Master of Information Systems Management, I’m passionate about solving problems strategically and intentionally. I enjoy seeing the big picture, learning from others’ perspectives, and collaborating across domains and disciplines. With training and experience spanning family studies, music, business, technology, and negotiation, I’ve developed strong listening skills, a creative mindset, and a genuine curiosity that I bring to every project and initiative I undertake. My goal is to improve processes and experiences, encourage and facilitate informed decisions, and build intentional products.

I entered tech from an arts background, which still shapes how I think about much of what I do. I studied Information Systems at BYU, but before that I was deep in music, spending years in trombone, choral groups, music production, and live accompaniment for dance courses, where I improvised across 13 instruments and tools to create something fresh, on-the-fly, every class.

In my Master of Information Systems Management, I focused on data, product management, cloud infrastructure, and AI. Before that, I had taught myself React and spent time exploring web development as a career path. Though I loved the user experience and visual side of this work, I felt drawn toward automation and other means of removing technological friction.

My general background is multidisciplinary in a way I have come to appreciate. Manufacturing, dental tech, web development, TA roles, study abroad, education, and music have all quietly shaped how I think. At BYU, I also studied abroad with about 30 other students from my Information Systems program, traveling the United Kingdom, Italy, and Switzerland to examine everyday applications of technology and the Fourth Industrial Revolution in real settings. This busy travel schedule taught me as much about the world and technology as it did about adaptability, low ego, and curiosity in relationship with my peers.

I took a pretty broad approach to undergrad, minoring in Family Life and taking courses in anthropology, yoga, philosophy, religious text analysis, and interior design. One of my favorite subjects was negotiation, which changed how I approach conflicts between ideas or individuals. I also sang for two years in BYU's selective choral program, first in the BYU Men's Chorus and then Concert Choir. Outside of coursework, I was involved in the Mindfulness and Meditation Club, Introverts in Business, UX Design Association, AI in Business Society, Association for Information Systems, and Product Management Association. I also enjoyed international film showings, where I was exposed to perspectives from and about other cultures.

The through-line in how people describe me has been that I listen well and stay genuinely curious. I think before I speak, I care about getting details right, and I am genuinely interested in the people I work with. If there is anything I’m most proud of, it’s that I have an open mind with high standards.

home.tldr: My approach is informed by my diverse experience, ranging from live improvised music to family studies. I bring this creative, empathic, and collaborative perspective to my work. I enjoy asking questions and uncovering unknowns to build solutions that make information easier to engage with.

handshakeLine: Working on something meaningful? I'd love to connect.

connect.heading: Let's connect
paragraphs: Reach out if you want a second pair of eyes and ears, need someone to test a product, or are hiring for a role where you think I could make a difference.
I'm always open unconventional ideas. Or maybe you're doing the same-old exceptionally? Would love to help with that too.
Send me a message via email or LinkedIn.

timeline.label: Earned Eagle Scout. The project required planning, leadership, follow-through, and sewing skills, supporting patients at the Intermountain Health Utah Valley Hospital.

Full-time Volunteer Representative for The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints in Albuquerque, NM for two years. A peer later said he never heard me speak a scripted word. I was serious about being authentic, thinking for myself, and speaking the people's language. I was also thorough and responsible with my use and adoption of our new technology tools.
Joined Brigham Young University Department of Dance as a Contemporary Dance Accompanist, improvising live music for 15–75 dancers across 4 years using 13 instruments and music software.

I started my Bachelor of Science in Information Systems program at Brigham Young University. I deliberately chose an unfamiliar path to challenge myself. I took many opportunities to expand my perspective, such as studying abroad in the United Kingdom, Switzerland, and Italy, taking diverse elective courses, and earning a minor in Family Life.

As a Web Developer at Brigham Young University, built and maintained pages for the Arts' websites, PR, and inclusivity initiatives. I also helped them diagnose and plan to improve their website storage issues.
First Teaching Assistant role at BYU Marriott School of Business. I facilitated answering questions and presenting reviews for 60 students covering Python, PostgreSQL, Django, HTML/CSS, and OOP.
INTEX 5-day project at BYU: consolidated and normalized 7 data sources, built a working web app in C#, ASP.NET, and AWS, and presented a fully functional archeological fieldwork data entry system.

As a WordPress Developer at BYU College of Fine Arts and Communications, I audited 50+ undocumented workflow files, cut storage 19% across 8 department sites, and trained my replacement. I also maintained and built webpages for the Arts' websites, PR, and inclusivity initiatives
Studied abroad across the United Kingdom, Switzerland, and Italy, where 30 students and I saw everyday uses of technology and the progression of tech into the Fourth Industrial Revolution.

Consultant Intern for The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints. For this project, I researched data sources for a world crisis information management tool, brainstormed strategic options, and prototyped a simple solution to show the pros and cons of the initial approach pursued by the team.
Graduated with a Bachelor of Science in Information Systems from Brigham Young University. Received the IS Alumni Choice Award for integrity.
Full Stack Development Intern at OCSC, where I shipped 20+ full-stack changes to a production SaaS web application in six months.

UX and Web Developer at The LTN Foundation, where I led development of a responsive React site, designed and normalized a relational database, and clarified the organization's branding and information architecture.
Partnered with a local business to design and test agentic AI systems built with LangChain and strategic reasoning.
As a Data & Systems Teaching Assistant at BYU Marriott, I taught 50+ graduate students Excel statistics, Tableau, data lifecycle, and SQL; debugged student work across five languages in TA labs.
Delivered AI B2B growth recommendations at the BYU Marriott Case Competition, and earned Best of Room.

Master of Information Systems Management, BYU Marriott School of Business. Emphasis in Data Engineering and Business Intelligence. Coursework: Agentic AI Systems, Data Engineering (Python, dbt, Snowflake), Data Science (R, scikit-learn), Cloud Engineering (AWS, Terraform).
Engineered a Python extraction pipeline processing 6.7M+ words of YouTube transcript data into AI-ready structured context.

Capstone project at BYU Marriott: built out core functionality of I AM, a React app with AI powered by Amazon Bedrock's models and knowledge base capabilities to allow content creators to scale connection with their audience while matching voice and brand in an authentic way.

Applied AI group project: designed and built MicroDeck, a Flutter-based habit-initiation app with user-authored micro-action cards and short timers. We developed rapidly with iterative design and testing, market research,user feedback, and agentic, spec-driven development workflows.

projects.name: YouTube Transcript Pipeline
projects.objective: Hours of spoken content in YouTube playlists are locked in the UI; not queryable or exportable.
projects.tradeOff: Local pipeline for fine-grained control over transcript data, allowing playlist ingestion, SQL querying, bronze/silver data layers, and idempotent transformations.
projects.previewAlt: YouTube Transcript Pipeline CLI and DuckDB workflow

projects.name: Capstone Project - I AM
projects.objective: Content creators with a large audience struggle to connect with the whole of their audience on an individual level, and current solutions to this are robotic and impersonal.
projects.tradeOff: Matched a creator's voice and brand with Amazon Bedrock models & knowledge base, and refined core user experience.
projects.previewAlt: I AM product demo video thumbnail on YouTube

projects.name: Soundwatch
projects.objective: Morning routines with multiple timed activities require repeatedly starting timers or watching a clock.
projects.tradeOff: Stopwatch tracks elapsed time with interval audio cues to allow presence and focus.
projects.previewAlt: Soundwatch interval timer running in the browser

projects.name: YouTube KPI Framework
projects.objective: I was tasked with measuring a digital product's performance, by creating a KPI framework that would strategically measure the product and its valuable outcomes (not just outputs).
projects.tradeOff: KPI framework based on YouTube platform, with metrics aimed at measuring real value, rather than vanity metrics.
projects.previewAlt: YouTube KPI Framework Canva design overview

projects.name: MicroDeck
projects.objective: Individuals with ADHD, executive dysfunction, or digital burnout struggle to bridge the intention-behavior gap; trackers and blockers often add friction instead of helping them start.
projects.tradeOff: Minimalist mobile habit-initiation app with user-authored micro-action cards and 2-minute timers, avoiding streaks, history, and intoxicating gamification.
projects.previewAlt: MicroDeck iOS habit initiation app concept

projects.name: Midi Randomizer
projects.objective: Fresh ideas can be limited by prior behavior and inputs, as well as current imagination. Machines imitate randomness in a way that can surprise and provide value to humans. My music production tools were lacking in easy randomization.
projects.tradeOff: Browser-based MIDI randomization tool for unpredictable patterns, built with Google's AI Studio and refined with Cursor.
projects.previewAlt: Midi Morph browser MIDI GUI on GitHub Pages

projects.name: Calendar Dashboard
projects.objective: Google Calendar doesn't show total time spent across different calendars, making it impossible to compare calendar plans against my time allocationgoals for each calendar.
projects.tradeOff: Google Apps Script syncs event data/durations in Sheets, computes calendar time ratios, and feeds a Looker Studio dashboard.
projects.previewAlt: Looker Studio calendar time allocation dashboard

projects.name: Voice-First Portfolio Site
projects.objective: Recruiters spend about a minute per candidate; most portfolios lead with flashy UI or undifferentiated GitHub links—not a clear read on how someone thinks, ships, and communicates.
projects.tradeOff: Refactored React portfolio into Astro with a json-based source of truth for copy.
projects.previewAlt: Voice-first portfolio site homepage with featured projects grid
